config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja oleh kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari perlindungan terhadap martabat, kebebasan, dan keamanan tiap orang. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh prinsip keadilan agar tidak menjadi alasan untuk menindas kelompok kecil atau suara yang tidak populer. Bagi saya, prioritas yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan masyarakat di atas individu secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because that can easily become a justification for suppressing ordinary people. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why many people value social stability and collective goals, but I also know that when individual rights are treated as secondary too often, abuse and silence can follow. A society is strongest when it protects both public order and the dignity, safety, and basic freedoms of each person. So I would say the relationship should be balance, not priority in one direction.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena menurut saya kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Pengalaman di Indonesia menunjukkan bahwa atas nama “kepentingan umum” sering ada risiko suara minoritas, kebebasan berpendapat, atau hak warga kecil diabaikan, padahal justru di situlah keadilan diuji. Memang benar masyarakat butuh ketertiban dan tujuan bersama, tetapi itu harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar yang jelas supaya tidak berubah jadi pembenaran untuk menekan orang. Jadi bagi saya, yang tepat bukan prioritas mutlak untuk masyarakat, melainkan keseimbangan yang tegas dengan perlindungan hak individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support giving society automatic priority over the individual, because that makes it too easy to excuse pressure, conformity, and silence. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand the appeal of collective order and social stability, but I have also seen how quickly “for the common good” can become a way to dismiss ordinary people’s rights and concerns. Public interests matter, but they should not be used to override basic dignity, freedom, and fair treatment unless there is a truly strong and limited reason. For me, the better principle is balance with clear boundaries, not a blanket rule that society always comes first.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang sebenarnya merugikan warga kecil, minoritas, atau orang yang suaranya tidak kuat. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi justru harus dijaga dengan batas yang tegas: hak dasar tidak boleh digeser sembarangan. Jadi, menurut saya, yang benar adalah keseimbangan yang ketat, bukan prioritas mutlak untuk masyarakat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize order, stability, and collective goals, but I also know that once “the public interest” becomes a blanket excuse, ordinary people can be pushed aside very easily. The rights of the individual are not a luxury; they are the boundary that keeps society from becoming arbitrary or oppressive. A good society should protect the common good without sacrificing basic dignity, freedom, and fair treatment.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
